explanation:
**Normal health values** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. These ranges are for guidance only—your personal medical history always takes priority.

## How It's Calculated

Maximum 400mg per day for healthy adults = 4 cups of American coffee or 8 espresso shots.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking against typical ranges and alerting you when results fall outside reasonable limits.

## Normal Reference Values

| Parameter | Normal Adult Range |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60-100 bpm |
| Body Temperature | 36.1-37.2°C |
| Blood Oxygen (SpO2) | 95-100% |
| Fasting Blood Glucose | 70-100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL Cholesterol | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL Cholesterol | >40 (M) / >50 (F) |

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions—medical, financial, or legal—consult a specialized professional. Values are current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much caffeine is safe per day?
faq.a: For healthy adults, 400mg per day is the maximum safe limit—about 4 cups of American coffee or 8 espresso shots.

faq.q: What's the difference in caffeine between coffee, tea, and energy drinks?
faq.a: An 8 oz cup of brewed coffee contains ~95-200mg caffeine, black tea has ~25-50mg, and energy drinks vary widely from 80-300mg per serving.
Pregnant women should limit caffeine to 200mg per day or less. Always consult your doctor about your specific situation.

faq.q: Can too much caffeine affect sleep?
faq.a: Yes. Caffeine consumed within 4-6 hours of bedtime can disrupt sleep. Individual sensitivity varies significantly.
Some antidepressants, beta-blockers, and thyroid medications can interact with caffeine. Always inform your doctor about your caffeine consumption.

faq.q: Is 400mg of caffeine per day actually safe?
faq.a: Yes, for most healthy adults. This limit is based on extensive research and recommended by major health organizations (FDA, WHO).
The American Academy of Pediatrics recommends children avoid caffeine entirely. Teens should limit intake to under 100mg per day.

faq.q: Do people build tolerance to caffeine?
faq.a: Yes, regular users develop tolerance over time. Taking occasional breaks can help reset your caffeine sensitivity.
